title: Cross Section of Spinal Cord Diagram
topic: spinal cord cross-section and nerve roots
description: A simplified schematic cross-section of the spinal cord showing the central butterfly-shaped gray matter and peripheral white matter. It illustrates the functional separation of the spinal nerve roots: the dorsal root carrying afferent sensory neurons (with ganglia) and the ventral root carrying efferent motor neurons.
clinical_notes: Understanding the separation of dorsal (sensory) and ventral (motor) roots is vital for OTs treating spinal cord injuries. In incomplete lesions, such as anterior cord syndrome, ventral motor function is lost while dorsal sensory proprioception is preserved, drastically altering the rehabilitation approach and adaptive equipment needs.
